text: Chemist here.  One of the complicating factors with Circumin in turmeric is that it absorbs light very well and can  reversibly attach to lots of proteins in the body.  This gives lots of false positives in the various in vitro assays that biologists use to try and sort out its mechanism of action.  There is at least one good study in rodents showing that it can reduce inflammation in vivo, but  when that came out (12ish?) years ago, nobody really understood how it was working.

text: I make a super tasty vegetable bread (kind of like a huge vegetable bhaji) that is packed with turmeric and spices. Simply grate and drain a couple of carrots and courgettes, fry off a red onion and several cloves of garlic until soft. Add 2 tbsp of turmeric and 1 tbsp of cumin to the pan along with the grated veg. Give it a good stir. Make up a thick batter with chickpea flour, olive oil and water and when smooth, pour the veg into the batter. Once mixed, pour over a lined baking tray and bake in the oven for about 30 mins. It's great served with a fresh salad and very filling.

title: Why Turmeric Is Actually Pointless 👉
text: Studies on turmeric show that its benefits aren't strictly related to absorption levels. In fact, adding black pepper to increase bioavailability can actually decrease its effectiveness because less turmeric stays in the gut, where it has the most impact. Turmeric supports beneficial gut bacteria like Lactobacillus reuteri, which is why its primary benefits are related to the microbiome. So, you don’t need to increase absorption with pepper, as the real value of turmeric is in how it nurtures gut health.

text: I've probably commented on this subject before but i'll do it again here,   About 15 years ago I discovered a little book called "Folk Medicine" by a Vermont country doctor D.C. Jarvis published in early-mid 1900s.   He didn't know the science at the time but discovered through experimentation exactly what Dr. Berg is talking about - ACV can balance pH.   He even used it on farm animals and dogs who recovered from various ailments.  Now us permaculture homesteaders and backyard farmers use ACV for everything including the chickens!  Anyhoo,  I've been taking a tablespoon of ACV with a spoonful of local HONEY for 15 yrs and have not had a cold or flu and rarely a sniffle since.  (My chickens are healthy too - no illness or mites).  Local honey prevents allergies and I've used it for hangovers and to prevent food poisoning (especially fish) he recommends having a little ACV before dinner.  I've also had diarrhea a couple of times when I forgot to use ACV as prophylactic so I head straight for the ACV and it resolves it.  No hanging over the toilet all night after a party or after eating tainted conch on a sailing cruise or tainted lettuce, lol.    It's a sweet book, easy to read, out of print but available in used bookstores.  And there's an amusing(?) 1960  New York Times story when his formula called "Honegar" was being sold as natural remedy but was seized by the FDA because the formulation and risks were not verified with the product, lol

text: Since studies make the news constantly, it is great that you are here to provide clarity; and a few hundred thousand people clearly appreciate your work. title: My Supplement Routine - PhD in Molecular Medicine Explains
text:
A great topic would be: how you evaluate supplement brands, particularly for quality and whether you trust they include the claimed ingredients. Even if you don't want to specifically endorse any brands as trustworthy or high quality, you could at least discuss how you decide for yourself. What information do you look for and where?  I always worry that, even if research on various supplemental molecules is valid, the supplement industry is rife with shoddy practices if not sometimes outright fraud, so I could be wasting my money anyway. Or if you don't worry about this much, you could explain why. And perhaps it varies by type of supplement.

[Edit: it does seem "Life Extension" is a brand you trust -- couldn't hurt to have a vid discussing your reasons if not an endorsement per se. More like "with this brand, I'm pretty confident I'm getting what I'm paying for (at a reasonable price?) and here's why..."]

title: Supplements I recommend as a doctor (and the ones I don’t)
text:
Recommended Supplements:
•  Vitamin D: Supports bone health, immune system, and brain function; many lack sufficient sun exposure or dietary intake.
•  Omega-3 (fish oil or algae): Benefits heart health and reduces inflammation; useful if fish consumption is low.
•  Vitamin B12: Essential for older adults, vegans, or those with absorption issues; prevents nerve and blood cell deficiencies.
•  Folic Acid (Folate): Critical for pregnant women; supports fetal development and prevents birth defects.
•  Magnesium: Aids energy, mood, and muscle function; often deficient in diets.
•  Fiber: Improves digestion and cholesterol control; helpful if diet lacks fiber.
•  Iron: Only for anemia or at-risk groups (e.g., pregnant women, heavy menstruation); boosts energy and blood production.

Not Recommended (or Only on Advice):
•  Multivitamins: No evidence for disease prevention; better through varied diet, risk of overdosing.
•  Calcium: Potential heart risks; prioritize diet and exercise for bones.
•  High-Dose Vitamin C: No added benefit for colds; excess excreted in urine.
•  Probiotics: Limited evidence for general health; not needed daily.
•  CoQ10, Turmeric, Glucosamine: Mixed evidence; often ineffective or unnecessary without specific conditions.
•  Collagen Powders: Little scientific support; mostly hype without solid benefits.

Reason: Supplements address dietary gaps, but a balanced diet is key. Overuse can be harmful (e.g., drug interactions). Always consult a doctor for personalized advice.

text:
Maybe the dosage a bit low on many supplements compared to studies, could be due to the fact that the effects of blueprint appears more or less after 2-3 months; 
Maybe your body need to accomodate and taking it everyday for 2-3 months contribute to a higher amount in your blood compared to what the studies generally monitor in term of duration. just an hypothesis.

personnally as a former soldier , now managing big state projects, I always did some sport routine with cardio and musculation.
-generally 20 min musculation early in the morning, 
- then shower and fresh bath, for anti depressive effect
-then 20 min bicycle (at maximum speed for hearth) to go to work (the same to go back at the end lf the day).

if I feel a bit tired I take 1-2 days stop in the week, or with only musculation or bicycle.


I add :
- hemp fiber supplements (because I don’t eat enough vegetables and fruits)
- antioxydants 
- some d3 vitamins sometimes (30% time)
- magnesium sometimes (30% time)
in France we don’t need chocolate and oil supplements, we got it everywhere with quality and  cheap

i always slept well (8-9 hours a night, without any problem) and I take a 20 min break at work after a very light meal (sardine box, banana…) 
I try to reduce my eat meal of 15% because of studies showing 2-5% slowing age 

now the bad things I do and try to improve :
-Mcdonald or fast food (1-2 times by week)
-party generally on friday night, with sometimes too much alcool which kill my saturday routine, and need to go back on sunday with difficulties and stress.

nobodys is perfect, and I am reducing bad habits regularly but slowly. 

i also have the chance to been born in one of the healthier place in france (Cantal) and My family live very long (more than 100 years, and my parents look 10 years younger without doing anything , and not much sport) so this is encouraging

title: Nutritionist Reviews The Blueprint Supplements
text:
At the end of the day, he publishes his data and he is aging very slowly. Sure, there are others like Siim Land who are “beating” him with far fewer supplements at a way lower annual cost, but his results are impressive.

I think it is notable that he already has far more money than he can probably spend in a lifetime. I’m sure he isn’t opposed to making more, but he has far less need to lie and manipulate than most supplement makers. And again, his results are impressive and it seems he genuinely wants to help others replicate them without excessive costs. 

I’ve tried to make similar supplement stacks to his and it is difficult to do so at the same price as him, particularly without sacrificing quality (on cacao for example). I would not be surprised if the margins on his supplements are near zero.

I feel people may not like him because of the broader social and economic issues that people relate to “Silicon Valley”/tech culture and people. Like Silicon Valley men are the modern “evil elites” so therefore Bryan Johnson is an evil vampire… maybe that isn’t you, but is certainly is the view of many who hate on him. Something to consider, but I think your points are still valid

title: Nutritionist Reviews The Blueprint Supplements
text:
His $2mil per year protocol is a bit misleading, because most of that is having a staff of doctors that can test on the fly if needed. His diet, exercise, and sleep routine is the real core that I am interested in. I actually do his protocol (minus the pills) the first 7 days of the month. Then after that I shift back to my regular diet which is mostly plant based. Cutting out processed foods is the big key though. 
As far as his products, I do buy the chocolate and olive oil. The reason is, it's near impossible to find out info on store bought chocolate and the amount of heavy metals in it. So in that sense, I'm trusting that his company has this process locked down. I hope I'm right. 
The olive oil - I checked his product vs some other single source products where I live and it's not actually that far off as far as price goes. 
I'm not into the longevity mix because I don't have a team of docs around me testing how any of this stuff is impacting me, so I don't see the point. I tend to only take supplements that I can get tested by my doc 2 times per years. So b12, d3, k2, magnesium, and a few others. 

What is really comes down to for his products and all the others for that matter, can I test it? If they really want to impress me, come up with a reasonably priced testing method for all of this stuff. I am not holding my breath for that. Too bad Theranos was run my a manipulative sociopath because that would have been a trillion dollar idea 😂

text:
Your time-saving hero:
1. Creatine (Don't buy gummies and other bullshit, because it contains less of actual creatine and is basically useless. Buy the default monohydrate creatine) = S tier for monohydrate creatine / D tier for creatine gummies and shit
2. Turkesteron (the one you buy in the stores might actually NOT contain Turkesteron in them (which is a steroid for plants and bugs) - useless and 99% placebo. = D tier
3. Beta-Hydroxy Beta-methylbutyric acid (HMB) - mostly useless for trainedlifter and POTENTIAL small lean muscle mass boost for newbies 0.5 - 1LB / 0.22 - 0.45 kg. (Video mentioned that you can POSSIBLY AS A NEW LIFTER get ~1 lb / 0.45 kg of lean mass for $20) = C tier 
4. Protein powder (9:41 for calculations). if you don't have enough protein in your body - you might see significant results (1 - 2 LB / 0.45 - 0.91 kg of lean mass over the next 3 months = $77 for 1 LB / 0.45 kg of lean mass), but if you already eat enough protein - you're shitting (literally) away your money = A tier for both Whey (animal) and Plant based protein (they're basically the same)
5. Mass gainer (aka protein shake with EXTRA calories). Extremely overpriced, just get your protein and eat a candy bar or something = B tier 
6. Fish Oil = $105 for 1 lb / 0.45 kg of lean mass. Good for overall health, pretty much useless for muscle growth = C tier. 
7. Testosteron Booster - Garbage and useless. Use (don't) steroids instead. Some of the boosters contains Boron, Magnesium and Zinc which is, just as fish oil, good for overall health - might help a bit with muscle mass if you don't have it enough in your body. = F tier / B tier if you are deficient in various micro-nutrients.

title: Ivermectin and COVID-19
text:
I saw a presentation on this topic which showed 4 different molecular biology paths for this substance having likely effect.
1) Interferes with the polymerase enzyme that the viruses use to replicate their RNA and thus make copies of themselves.
2) acts as a mild zinc ionophore to help bring more serum zinc inside a cell membrane  than the cell would normally take inside, which the zinc throws a proverbial monkey wrench into RNA virus replication too.
3) raises the pH of the cell's cytoplasm a little bit which also further hampers RNA virus replication
4) blocks importin α/β on the cell's nucleus membrane such that pathogenic proteins released by a virus after it invades the cell's outer membrane ,cannot break inside the nucleus also to corrupt the DNA inside. (Lots of official peer reviewed papers have been published on that exact topic).

These four things together..... well, I'm no molecular biologist /virologist.... just have a lowly BS CompSci degree, but even I can see from the undergrad biology classes I took in school, how this medicine stands a very high probability of being very useful here. And that's not even counting the mountain of observed excellent results that doctors all over the planet have witnessed and written about.

No fever or body aches after start of Ivermectin.  I was with her for the duration, taking basic precautions, and never became infected.  I am wondering if her use of Ivermectin provided me with some level of protection from the active virus.  She had no underlying health issues, with a weight of 135lbs.

title: Ivermectin and Covid-19:  We have an answer!
text: If the Elgazzar paper was included in the meta-study then the conclusions are suspect. Most of the other papers are also sloppy or simply too small. There’s a lot of really bad research out there and it’s very difficult to rely on their conclusions. Andrew Bryant might not be biased, but he didn’t do proper due diligence on this meta study. There are several other meta studies, and depending on which studies you include, the results can be positive or negative. The jury is still out but it’s still very speculative; there’s no good study so far that shows ivermectin is any good. Anecdotal stories really don’t count.

NOOOOO REALLLLY??????

title: Black Seed Oil: The 30-Day Adventure to Better Health - Usage, Benefits, and What to Expect!
text:
At the beginning of 2020, I found out that I have a grade IV glioblastoma multiforme, and was told I had 12-14 months to live. After reading up on GBMs, I realized that the estimate of 12-14 months I'd been given was a generous. While going through chemo and radiation treatments, the thought that 75% of those who have GBMs die within the first year. So I did many searches for additional treatments, preferably something that was all-natural. I quickly found black seed oil.

After reading a laundry list of conditions black seed oil helps to treat, and that it is touted to be "the cure for everything but death itself", I started taking it in April of 2020. Today is February 28th, 2024, over four years after my initial diagnosis, I am still here. While I know that this GBM will be the death of me, I absolutely believe that black seed oil has helped me get this far. I have a 5% chance of living to see 2025, and less than a 1% chance of seeing 2026. But I am in awe of the fact that I have been given this extra time.

title: Black Seed Oil: The 30-Day Adventure to Better Health - Usage, Benefits, and What to Expect!
text:
BLACK SEED OIL:
Let’s talk thymoquinone for those new to BSO… and for the BSO connoisseur our TQ % and the subsequent breakdown of that. Our oil is tested to exceed 1% in its natural state. We do not add thymoquinone or adulterate our oil (which is unfortunately a practice of some suppliers in the industry, just like they do with the honey scam).
As I strolled the isles of a favorite health market recently I noticed the explosion of so many things “BSO”… and shaking my head, as the majority are crap… so here’s the skinny on the fat…
Helpful tips as you enter our world of BSO…
First, if it comes in a capsule, I’m personally tossing it in the trash. Next, if the Nigella Sativa seeds are not from one of the counties listed below, also in the trash. Lastly, aside from using our BSO, Roby would add actual Nigella Sativa seeds, ground them in a coffee bean grounder to fine powder and steep like tea and drink. (I’m not yet willing to ruin my tea or coffee with it, LOL…). It’s nasty tasting but the health benefits, well, are described in many NIH articles.
In the world of supplements, you want to ensure the products you are investing in are non-adulterated and have no added solvents or in the case of BSO added TQ.
Here’s what to look for, what to stay away from, and how to start using black seed oil. Note that I will not be talking about BSO in capsule form. I do not recommend these as it’s quite easy to hide an inferior product inside a capsule and the quantity of capsules one would have to consume would just be ridiculous when the oil is so readily available.
Be wary of cheap brands. And not all cheap brands are cheap in cost. So know what to look for before you buy.
Seed origin: High quality natural black seeds come from where they grow best...Middle East, Turkey, Sudan, Egypt, and India.
Oil extraction method: It should be Slow Cold Pressed. No heat should ever be used in the extraction, as it will evaporate the more volatile compounds that are crucial to the healing property of BSO. Even a cold press with a fast process can generate enough heat to volitize those compounds we are looking to preserve in a quality BSO. Any BSO that has a red color or bitter taste should be suspected to have been exposed to heat during the processing. Likewise, do not purchase any product that has undergone a chemical extraction process. Benzene and other chemicals can be added to the processing to improve the oil yield, but in the process contaminate the BSO product.
Purity: This speaks for itself. When you pay for black seed oil you expect to get black seed oil. Pure means 100% pressed oil of the Nigella sativia seeds, completely free from any additive. Walk away from any BSO that is not 100% BSO (Read labels carefully).
Quality: To gain the benefit of those healing compounds in the skin of the black seed, choose an unrefined/unfiltered BSO. Sometimes you can even see tiny solids in the oil.
Color/Viscosity: BSO color should always range from light to medi

title: How to do a Full-Body Detox Cleanse #shorts
text:
Autophagy and detoxification are both concepts aimed at cleansing the body, but they function in fundamentally different ways.
While autophagy is a proven cellular self-cleaning mechanism, detox is often a marketing term for diets or regimens whose effectiveness is hardly supported by scientific evidence.
Here is a comparison of the two approaches:
1. Autophagy: The Cell's Recycling Program
• Definition: Autophagy (from the Greek for "self-consuming") is a biological process in which cells break down and recycle damaged, old, or defective components (proteins, cell organelles).
• Effect: It is a kind of "garbage collection" at the cellular level. It protects against cellular aging, reduces inflammation, and plays a role in the prevention of diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.
• Activation: Autophagy is primarily stimulated by nutrient deficiency, usually through fasting (from about 12-16 hours), intense exercise, or specific diets such as the ketogenic diet.
• Science: Nobel Prize-winning science (Yoshinori Ohsumi, 2016).
2. Detox: The term for "detoxification"
• Definition: Detox programs (juice cleanses, special teas) promise to rid the body of "waste products" and toxins.
• Effect: Usually short-term weight loss due to a calorie deficit and a feeling of freshness. However, there is no scientific evidence that specific products promote "detoxification."
• Activation: Often through abstaining from solid food and replacing it with juices.
• Science: The body detoxifies itself independently and continuously via the liver, kidneys, intestines, and lungs. There is no scientific evidence for the necessity of special "detox products."

title: The 10-Day Detox: How To Heal The Body Through A Diet & Lifestyle Reset | Dr. Mark Hyman
text:
Below is a summary:

Dr. Mark Hyman discusses his 10-day detox program, which is designed to reset the body by removing harmful foods and replacing them with beneficial ones. The program is based on the principle that food is medicine, providing not just energy but also instructions that program our biology. By eating the right foods, we can regulate gene expression, hormones, brain chemistry, the immune system, and the microbiome.

**Key Pillars of the 10-Day Detox Program:**

1. **Eat Real Food:** The diet focuses on consuming whole foods like non-starchy vegetables, good fats, fiber, and clean protein. This includes a variety of vegetables, avocados, olive oil, nuts, seeds, and proteins like grass-fed meat and organic plant-based options like tempeh.

2. **Remove Inflammatory Foods:** The detox involves eliminating foods that cause inflammation, such as processed foods, sugar, dairy, coffee, gluten, alcohol, and most grains and beans.

3. **Daily Habits:** Establishing a pattern of eating and living that puts the body back in rhythm is crucial. This includes eating within a 10 to 12-hour window and fasting overnight for 12 to 14 hours, as well as ensuring quality sleep and regular sleep patterns.

4. **Supplementation:** Dr. Hyman suggests that even with a perfect diet, supplementation is necessary due to the reduced nutrient density of modern food. Recommended supplements include a multivitamin, magnesium, vitamin D, and possibly fish oil.

**Program Steps:**

1. **Follow the Approved Food List:** For 10 days, stick to the foods recommended by the detox program.

2. **Commit to Daily Habits:** Choose a new eating window, avoid snacking before bed, and establish a consistent bedtime routine.

3. **Add Supplements:** While optional, supplements can support the detox process.

**Post-Detox Guidance:**

After completing the detox, reintroducing foods should be done carefully to avoid adverse reactions. Dr. Hyman advises continuing the principles of the detox if it yields positive results and transitioning to a more flexible diet, such as the pegan diet, over time.

**Conclusion:**

Dr. Hyman emphasizes the importance of listening to one's body and using the detox as a way to understand how food impacts health. He encourages doing the detox with a friend or group for accountability and better success rates.
author: @MrQuadcity

title: The 10-Day Detox: How To Heal The Body Through A Diet & Lifestyle Reset | Dr. Mark Hyman
text: 10 years ago, I spent 3 months eating nothing but raw vegetables, fruits, nuts and cooked beans for protein. Back then I suffered from many ailments from severe menstrual cramps, poor circulation, fatigue, constipation, always feeling cold, varicose & spider veins, allergies, the list goes on. I was in my 40s, not over weight, but really unhealthy; the majority of my food was processed, fried and alot of it was smothered in cheese and I rarely ate any vegetables. When I first went on this eating plan I thought I would always be hungry, but I was eating so much fiber that I had to force myself to eat because I never felt hungry. I had been on this new eating plan 3 days when my period came. My menstrual cramps normally felt like I was giving birth to my period every month, it was horrible, but just 3 days on my eating plan and my period came and went with no cramps. I couldn't believe it! That was my motivation to keep going. Within 3 weeks I noticed my hands and feet were never cold anymore, my nose which is always running and congested was clear and after 2 months my fatigue was gone and I had so much energy. I didn't originally plan to do 3 months, just wanted to see how how long I could do it for. To be honest it was hard to do, eating pretty much the same thing every day got boring. I basically grazed all day. I would pre-cut up vegetables and store them in the fridge for quick access. Avocados and bananas  I wld cut up last minute. The only reason I ended up "falling off the wagon" so to speak was because I was traveling over the summer to visit family & it became too hard to maintain the eating since I couldnt keep vegetables cold in the car for long periods of time. Fastforward 10 yrs and my circulation is getting worse now that I am older (50s) and I want to try the detox eating again. I would say if your able to do it cold turkey, that wld be great, if not then fill up your plate with vegetables, fruits, and nuts and a small bit of protein (fish, chicken, beans, etc.) and then tell yourself your reward is the protein if u can finish the veggies, fruit & nuts first. I want to get back to feeling healthy again so I can be around along time for my kids who I ended up having late in life. Good luck everyone, I am rooting for u! 🥰
author: @onewiththeunknown8434

title: The 10-Day Detox: How To Heal The Body Through A Diet & Lifestyle Reset | Dr. Mark Hyman
text:
Bob Roman's Summary: 10 day Reset! The Great Reset!

Here is a 10 point summary and outline of the key principles from the 10 Day Reset Diet video:

1. Eat real, whole foods - Food is medicine that programs your biology. Eat foods that reduce inflammation and toxicity.

2. Remove inflammatory/toxic foods - Get rid of processed foods, sugar, dairy, coffee, gluten, alcohol, grains, beans.

3. Eat lots of veggies, healthy fats, clean protein - Focus your diet around non-starchy veggies, avocados, olive oil, nuts/seeds, grass-fed/organic meats and fish.  

4. Don't restrict calories - Eat as much real food as you want, focus on food quality not quantity.

5. Time your eating - 12-14 hour overnight fast, no snacking before bed. Gives digestion a rest.

6. Prioritize sleep - Crucial for health, mood, cravings. Wind down/disconnect 1-2 hrs before bed.

7. Take supportive supplements - Multivitamin, magnesium, vitamin D, fish oil. Nutrient insurance.

8. Follow the approved foods list - Details good proteins, fats, veggies, fruits, oils, nuts, seeds, herbs/spices.

9. Add back foods slowly - Reintroduce foods one at a time over 3 days each to check reactions.

10. Listen to your body - It's the smartest doctor. The program works if you feel significantly better.

title: The DARK SIDE of Ashwagandha
text: 1:54 It’s not serotonin, it’s GABA. Ashwagandha is GABAergic. It enhances both GABA alpha and GABA beta1 significantly, the reason it’s calming and promotes sleep. The numbing effects have nothing to do with the plant’s “defence molecules”, as too much GABA activity for a prolonged time can have an emotion-numbing effect. Example: GABAergic medications such as benzodiazepines (e.g. alprazolam) and Gabapentinoids (e.g. pregabalin) can have numbing and emotional blunting effects at high doses after prolonged time.

title: The DARK SIDE of Ashwagandha
text: Hi Dr. Berg, it is nice to see this video because I have been a bit concerned about promoting ashwagandha... Yes, I have low blood pressure naturally, and I cannot take ashwagandha. I also try not to use any solanaceous plant products (nightshades), because they are naturally toxic. I study insects and I see that naturally in nature... Birds avoid most solanaceous and fabaceous (legumes)--feeding insects. Learning from wild animals is the best way to increase awareness of the natural compound/toxicity, etc. For example, I see most animals do not eat cucurbit skins (zucchini, squash, cucumber, etc.,i.e. Curcurbitaceae). Knowing basic biology and taxonomy also helps a lot to see the characteristics of each animal, plant, and other organism at the family or subfamily level--they tend to share characters. In other words, it is wise to build up knowledge on the basics.

text:
The sacred ayurvedic texts: the Charaka and the Sushruta Sambita document the use of “Ashwagandha” as one of the most potent adaptogen and anti-stress herb. The Sanskrit name “Ashwagandha” translates to mean “the smell of a horse” and is recognized as one of the most powerful herbs for its restorative benefits and for providing “the virility and strength of a stallion.”
The use of Ashwagandha as medicine to revitalize the body dates back to the teachings of the highly revered sage, Atreya Punarvasu, a royal physician. This strength builder herb has the lion’s share in the many ayurvedic formulation, including Ashwagandha Di Ich yam , and is also referred to as a “royal herb” widely prescribed for its potent stress-relieving aphrodisiac, and immuno-modulatory activities’

title: Is ashwagandha the new super herb?
text: As far as herbs go, there is a good deal of scientific research on ashwagandha showing physical and mental benefit, none of which was covered in this video.  what was covered is that when herbs become popular they can be unnecessarily added to products (often topical and cosmetic ones) where it has no benefit.  What herb hasn't been put into a shampoo or body cream as a marketing ploy after it achieved a consumer buzz?  The definition of adaptogens generally includes a lack of toxicity along with the ability to improve the body's ability to withstand greater levels of general stress.  To call a double martini an adaptogen is a stupid comparison and no herbalist, ayurvedic doctor, or even average consumer would make that kind of mistake.  This video was weak in content and spent little time discussing the actual herb.  Like the shampoo, this video seems an attempt to cash in by dropping Ashwagandha's name while using it as unrelated filler.

Great video, Thomas! Thought I would share some info regarding this virus based on my experience as an exercise physiologist and years of training in functional medicine. It's lengthy, but very informative for anyone that is interested.

Latest research is showing that younger adults are dealing with the virus better and more likely to recover because their innate immune system is very responsive. The innate immune system is your first line of defense and it is responsible for alerting the warriors (T-Cells) about the invader so they can go to battle and destroy the virus. If you are an older individual or someone with a less responsive innate immune system, then you have a greater chance for the virus to come in and further down regulate the innate immune system and sneak in the back door so to speak. By the time your (T-Cells) recognize the virus, it's too late as it's already significantly replicated throughout the body and more specifically the alveoli in your lungs. By this time your immune system goes haywire so to speak and it can overreact - a process called a cytokine storm and this causes extreme inflammation and it's what's causing people to succumb to the virus.

Latest research shows that up to 25% of people can have the virus and not show symptoms at all and never will. However, they are considered carriers and can spread the virus like wildfire. These are the people I believe who have a strong innate immune system and that is why it’s VERY important to treat everyone you come into contact with as though they have the virus as they can be a CARRIER and show NO symptoms at all! 

Here is some VERY important information on how to strengthen the innate part of your immune system.

Tissue Macrophages are your first line of defense and they need the following supplements so they can be very responsive when going to battle.

    Plenty of rest/sleep/prayer/deep breathing exercises. When you sleep your immune system goes to work. If you are under emotional stress, you activate the sympathetic nervous system which in turn puts you in fight or flight mode. In this mode your immune system is put on the back burner so to speak which in turn could allow the virus to run wild. Prayer is a direct communication with God and this in itself can calm the sympathetic nervous system and allow your immune system to recover. If you are a non believer, then meditation will work - although in my opinion connecting with source is optimal. Deep breathing is a must. Search online for how to do this properly as it makes a huge difference in lung function and calming the sympathetic nervous system.
    Drink plenty of warm liquids OR very cold liquids if you have a fever.
    Vitamin D3/K2 10,000 to 40,000IU per day of D3 is needed. 40,000IU of D3 can be taken for up to 5 days if you have the virus.
    400-600 magnesium orotate which is needed to convert D3 to it’s active form. This is a must otherwise your magnesium stores will deplete and you may experience

title: Vitamin D, Immune System & SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) | Mechanism of Vit D Immune Regulation & Overview
text:
Vit D serum levels vary by latitude, weather, lifestyle, body size, body fat levels, starting serum levels, co-factor levels, etc.  Therefore a single fixed dose given to a variety of folks will have a variety of results.

The most useful D variable versus any outcome is the serum level.  

The next methodological issue is the outcome criteria...... rickets, heart disease, etc.  Each disease may respond to different serum levels.  Rickets might be prevented at fairly low levels (e.g. 20-30 ng/ml) while infectious diseases might require much higher levels.

Since natural levels at the equator are 40-60 ng/ml, a safe serum target is 50 ng/ml.

The final issue is loading dose versus maintenance dose.  Someone with a low D level will need a temporary high dose to raise their levels while a maintenance dose can be lower (Dr Heaney suggests 35IU/pound of body weight daily).  The loading dose (see also Holick re "filling the tank") could be 10,000 iu/day for 4-5 weeks, re-test, adjust.  

If serum levels do not respond to dosing, then suspect a co-factor deficiency, usually magnesium.  See also co-factors.

Cheers.

text: Controlled studies show vitamin K2 as MK-7 has no toxicity even at astronomically high doses (in rats), and a correct human dose has more to do with individual  tolerance for huge amounts of natto as the preferred medium for the vitamin.  I found I needed to increase my daily dose of vitamin K2 as MK-7 rather slowly over a period of six months, to prevent flatulence, increasing from 100 mcg to 300 mcg over the six months, and I do feel great about that supplement increase as I also increased my daily dose of vitamin d3 over the same six months from 50 to 80mcg.  Given my choice of vitamin d3 is vegan, I always take fat-soluble supplements with a meal including some kind of fat or oil, omega 3s, olive oil, cheese, butter or walnuts.  My magnesium is taken in the form of magnesium glycinate only, a 200 mg dose, evenings only, with at least one large walnut or small bag of potato chips for fat, and a few swigs of kombucha for acid.  I’m super fussy about my kombucha: I drink two bottles of GT Synergy brand kombucha every day, at least one with my main meal, and a couple of ounces for a touch of acid later in the evening with the magnesium glycinate, and I am even fussy about my flavors of GT Synergy: guava, cranberry, lemon berry, California citrus, and strawberry.  Remarkable as I was writing, I heard “synergistic and thirsty!”  GT Synergy kombucha is a medicinal probiotic beverage that relieves thirst and tastes like champagne, highly recommend.  (Also, because I love cheese so much and believe I need the calcium, I go over average on my dose of vitamin K2 as M-7, and under current radar on my vitamin d dose, hope that explanation helps).  Let’s keep sharing our tips!

title: Is CBD Oil the Miracle Cure for Everything?  What Does Research Show. Find Out Now!
text: 3:15 Great discussion, but it's worth noting that while definitive large-scale RCTs on CBD are limited, there are studies showing promising results. For example, research in *The Journal of Pain* highlights CBD's potential in pain management, though outcomes vary based on dosage and condition. Similarly, small RCTs have shown CBD's promise for anxiety, as noted in *The Journal of the American Pharmacists Association*. The mixed results reflect a need for more rigorous studies, not a lack of efficacy. Let’s keep pushing for better research to uncover CBD's full potential!

text: I've been experimenting with CBD for a couple of years. My findings are that you probably shouldn't be taking it as a regular dose. It works best for me as a kind of "rescue remedy". I suffer from IBS and mild anxiety, and I find CBD very effective at treating both, if I just use it when I feel like I need it. It doesn't seem to make me sleepy. One thing about this video that rang true for me was that you need high doses (100-200mg) for it to be effective. 5mg is not going to do anything for you if you're feeling anxious.

title: Lethal drug or beneficial supplement? Here’s the truth about kratom
text: My personal experience- don’t take kratom lightly. I didn’t have chronic pain or a mood disorder. I was just a guy that quit alcohol due to hangovers and was looking for another social lubricant that didn’t give me the downside of alcohol (hangovers, impairment, etc). I thought I struck gold when I discovered kratom. The problem is my mix of innocence and ignorance were a dangerous combination. After a few months I was having it daily, because the feeling was that good. I’d say within 4 months the regular use caught up to me and I started having terrible gas, and my gastrointestinal system just wasn’t working right. My face looks a bit older and I have some dark pigmentation. But it’s not terrible yet. I’ve since stopped after about a year since the first time I tried it. If you have a medical condition I get it, but I advise against using kratom to just party and certainly advise against daily use especially if you’re just looking for a good buzz. I suppose the same can be said for alcohol. But there’s a lot of rhetoric around kratom still that says “it’s all natural”, “it’s just an herb”, etc and that is misleading. There’s nothing out there that makes you feel that good that isn’t a drug.

title: Lethal drug or beneficial supplement? Here’s the truth about kratom
text:
I was addicted to FDA approved pain pills, benzos and muscle relaxers for 11 years straight. 

I got fired from chronic pain management in April 2019. After that, I went to the streets and started doing cocaine while I was searching for a new pain management doctor. 

In June 2019, whilst in cocaine withdrawal, I watched that documentary “A Leaf of Faith.” My journey with Kratom began the very next day. 

Kratom shut that “I need one more snort, one more pill, one more escape” noise off in my brain. It allowed me to work on my whys of addiction. I have not been back to pain management in 5 years. 

I am a functioning member of society again. I am a thriving mother again. I am no longer a burden to my kids. I will be forever grateful and thankful that I accidentally found lab tested plain leaf powder Kratom. 

I now pass on my journey, wisdom and science to help those struggling with addiction, chronic pain, anxiety and depression. 

Yours truly, Kratom advocate/activist from Colorado est. 2019.

title: 2-Minute Neuroscience: Kratom
text:
I was addicted to FDA approved pain pills, benzos and muscle relaxers for 11 years straight, 2008-2019.

I got fired from chronic pain management in April 2019 because I didn’t show up for a per contract pill count. I was 11 pills short and my drug dealers didn’t have any pink oxy 10’s.  

After that, I went to the streets and started doing cocaine while I was searching for a new pain management doctor. 

In June 2019, whilst in cocaine withdrawal, I watched that documentary “A Leaf of Faith.” My journey with Kratom began the very next day. 

Kratom shut that “I need one more snort, one more pill, one more escape” noise off in my brain. It allowed me to work on my whys of addiction. I have not been back to pain management in 6 years. 

I am a functioning member of society again. I am a thriving mother again. I pay taxes again. I am no longer a burden to my kids. I am now a first time grandma. 

I will be forever grateful and thankful that I accidentally found lab-tested whole-leaf powder Kratom. 

I now pass on my journey, wisdom and science to help those struggling with addiction, chronic pain, anxiety and depression. 

Yours truly, Kratom advocate/activist from Colorado est. 2019.

There is a street drug called Krypton which mixes karatom with O-desmethyltramadol.  Some deaths have occurred because of the street drug. title: What is Kratom? | Complete Drug History and Uses | W/ Dr. B
text: Really great informative video. It was exactly what I was looking for. I like to know where it comes from and it’s purported benefits or uses. I am 34 years old and just try to for the first time because I’ve been visiting Florida for a few weeks and I was driving around and noticed they had these kava bars so I checked out a few. While doing so they each had drinks made with Kratom as well. So I tried this seltzer type drink they had. I got a euphoric feeling definitely a more creative mind. I would say it energize me. Plus I am very knowledgeable about many different herbs. I’ve been studying herbalism for several years and I have a apothecary. However I didn’t know anything about Kratom. Last night unfortunately I drank just a little bit of what I had left of that drink around 10 PM at night and I was wired all night and couldn’t sleep till about maybe 5 AM and I feel like shit today. I felt super hyper alert. I also ate some dark chocolate last night so that could’ve added to it as dark chocolate is known to have caffeine. I really smoke marijuana anymore because it also gives me insomnia. I enjoy the creativity it creates and helps to open my mind however not sleeping at all it’s usually not worth it. I had a feeling that it had some type of stimulant affect because it is a leaf therefore almost similar to a Tea. The bar that I went to also had several different colors such as red yellow or green and one was said to be more energizing and uplifting and the other was to be more relaxing which was the red color which is what I tried. I think that is possibly misleading or incorrect because just like marijuana whether it’s sativa or Indica despite the purported different affects of relaxation or mind stimulation it is still the same substance. Just a different strain. So with Kratom I believe no matter what if you are hardwired a certain way it’s going to affect you in that way. I see comments of people getting off Suboxone for this drug or herb. Good for them. I’ve never had a problem with addiction to opiates. I was looking for it for help with inflammation and arthritis as I fractured my hand in several places and never healed correctly. I also have pretty bad social anxiety and I’ve been in a severe depression because of other traumatic experiences I had. No matter WHAT effects I guarantee these are not as bad as antidepressants or anxiety medication such as clonzapam or trazadone or sertraline. That stuff makes me sooo tried and yawning. No matter what, if it’s natural in its natural form like kratom. As long as ppl don’t abuse it it’s okay in moderation. It’s those teenagers taking handfuls and constantly that are idiots trying to get messed up off of it that would cause ill effects. Which tbh would’ve probably been me in HS

title: What is Kratom? | Complete Drug History and Uses | W/ Dr. B
text:
I first started taking kratom about 18 months ago as something to help with focus in my job. I was told that white kratom was what would help while green and red helped more with pain and sleep respectively. Knowing that milligram dosage might actually affect what you feel also makes sense. Such as a high dose curbing opioid cravings while load dosages have a more stimulant effect is reminiscent of coffee/caffeine (of it is related) can increase focus and energy at higher doses but can have a calming effect at low doses. 

At the moment it is on the FDA watch list meaning there is some risk that it will become a controlled substance the way ephedra, camphor and sassafras did back in the '90s. There's always some room for abuse but the fact that it helps both people who suffer from chronic pain and or opioid addiction is profound and makes it prone for Big Pharma to seek to control much the way it did with marijuana, and soon psilocybin mushrooms.

title: What You Should Know Before Taking Kratom
text: Great supplement for addiction clearly, when taken properly. But, be VERY, VERY, VERY careful how far you go especially if you are not experienced in how dangerous addiction can be. Kratom is in fact very addictive if used incorrectly, and can have some pretty insane side effects, especially when abusing the liquid extracts. Key word, abuse. I used every day for 2 years, i was so confident in its safety and little side effects, i became unaware of how often or how much i was taking. it became second nature. Then it turned into $20 a day on an extract in the morning, followed by 8-10 spoon fulls after lunch up until bed time, sometimes more. the level of psychological damage the abuse has caused me is relatively extreme, although it is bearable once through the withdrawals. that being said, the withdrawals from this substance are far easier to deal with than other pain and anxiety medications, which is why it is proffered amongst the community. i do believe this is a miracle supplement, when taken correctly. But, moderation should not be left in the hands of an addict. Keep this in mind, if you don’t NEED it, don’t start it

title: What You Should Know Before Taking Kratom
text: I use it often. Have so for over a decade.  And I’m a practicing physician in a thriving clinic. The dangers associated with kratom are primarily limited to its addictive nature. title: What You Should Know Before Taking Kratom
text: I started taking Kratom about 6yrs ago to get off Suboxone "which I did, but I just traded 1 for the other". About 2yrs ago I started feeling weird. Kinda spacey & that feeling got worse & worse as time went on. Then about a yr into feeling like that I started having really high blood pressure. I mean it was terrifying waking up in the morning with blood pressure 180/100. I cut my Kratom use down till I finally got off & my blood pressure is no longer an issue. I also had breathing problems, racing heart beat, extreme dry mouth, loss of appetite & a cpl others. Now I will say I was taking a very high dose probably 8-10 capsules every 4hrs. Like I said I tapered myself off and when I got around taking 4-5 capsules every 4hrs is when all my symptoms disappeared. I'm only putting this out there cause it was terrifying I had no idea what was going on, my doctor prescribed me blood pressure meds which only elevated my blood pressure. That's when I realized it's the Kratom.

title: What You Should Know Before Taking Kratom
text: From my experience, it doesn't take long to build up a tolerance and snag some really bad withdrawals.  I have difficulty understanding how people use it daily but I can see it being good if used in conjunction with other methods.  Say 2- 3x a week using kratom and 4x a week delta 8 to manage a chronic condition.  I don't think the issue is black or white.  And I think it can be really helpful.  But it can get out of hand quickly if not (somewhat) closely monitored.  And of course, depends on medication interactions and your own personal biology so experiment but do so with caution.  If you feel even a twinge of the next day weirdness - ease up.  Its not worth that restless legs/arms + extreme insomnia + breathing issues + unique version of depression (and I struggle with really bad chronic depression/anxiety...this was its own unique form of torment).

title: What You Should Know Before Taking Kratom
text: I am 34 years old and just try to for the first time because I’ve been visiting Florida for a few weeks and I was driving around and noticed they had these kava bars so I checked out a few. While doing so they each had drinks made with Kratom as well. So I tried this seltzer type drink they had. I got a euphoric feeling definitely a more creative mind. I would say it energize me. Plus I am very knowledgeable about many different herbs. I’ve been studying herbalism for several years and I have a apothecary. However I didn’t know anything about Kratom. Last night unfortunately I drank just a little bit of what I had left of that drink around 10 PM at night and I was wired all night and couldn’t sleep till about maybe 5 AM and I feel like shit today. I felt super hyper alert. I also ate some dark chocolate last night so that could’ve added to it as dark chocolate is known to have caffeine. I really smoke marijuana anymore because it also gives me insomnia. I enjoy the creativity it creates and helps to open my mind however not sleeping at all it’s usually not worth it. I had a feeling that it had some type of stimulant affect because it is a leaf therefore almost similar to a Tea. The bar that I went to also had several different colors such as red yellow or green and one was said to be more energizing and uplifting and the other was to be more relaxing which was the red color which is what I tried. I think that is possibly misleading or incorrect because just like marijuana whether it’s sativa or Indica despite the purported different affects of relaxation or mind stimulation it is still the same substance. Just a different strain. So with Kratom I believe no matter what if you are hardwired a certain way it’s going to affect you in that way. I see comments of people getting off Suboxone for this drug or herb. Good for them. I’ve never had a problem with addiction to opiates. I was looking for it for help with inflammation and arthritis as I fractured my hand in several places and never healed correctly. I also have pretty bad social anxiety and I’ve been in a severe depression because of other traumatic experiences I had. No matter WHAT effects I guarantee these are not as bad as antidepressants or anxiety medication such as clonzapam or trazadone or sertraline. That stuff makes me sooo tried and yawning. No matter what, if it’s natural in its natural form like kratom. As long as ppl don’t abuse it it’s okay in moderation. It’s those teenagers taking handfuls and constantly that are idiots trying to get messed up off of it that would cause ill effects. Which tbh would’ve probably been me in HS. I was smoking weed all the time, abusing prescription drugs like vicodin and adderall. But def not an addict now and alll the ppl that say it’s helped them kick alcohol or heroin or suboxone I have Zero compassion for. If your only excuse is emotional use as in those vices gtfoh. Most addicts are entitled rich kids that haven’t had jobs so they’ve been priv

title: Kratom! A Natural Remedy for Pain, Anxiety, and More
text:
I was addicted to FDA approved pain pills, benzos and muscle relaxers for 11 years straight. 

I got fired from chronic pain management in April 2019. After that, I went to the streets and started doing cocaine while I was searching for a new pain management doctor. 

In June 2019, whilst in cocaine withdrawal, I watched that documentary “A Leaf of Faith.” My journey with Kratom began the very next day. 

Kratom shut that “I need one more snort, one more pill, one more escape” noise off in my brain. It allowed me to work on my whys of addiction. I have not been back to pain management in over 5 years. 

I am a functioning member of society again. I am a thriving mother again. I am no longer a burden to my kids. I will be forever grateful and thankful that I accidentally found lab-tested whole-leaf powder Kratom. 

I now pass on my journey, wisdom and science to help those struggling with addiction, chronic pain, anxiety and depression. 

Yours truly, Kratom advocate/activist from Colorado est. 2019.

title: Benefits of Green Kratom after 30 Days
text:
I am a 61 year old guy who takes Kratom everyday up here where I live, which is Spokane, Wash. In my home state of Alabama, Kratom was outlawed by the state legislature back in 2016.
     I take 17-20 500mg capsules each day, but I divide that number by morning and evening doses. I also take ashwagandha in the mornings.
     This combination does give a calming, slightly sedative effect, but it doesn’t get you “high”. Kratom can give you an opiate effect, but that is in large doses and you are really screwing up your liver. So do it in moderation and with common sense; I suggest you see your doctor before you go on any dose.

title: Nebulizing Hydrogen Peroxide for Respiratory Health
text: Please, correct me if I’m wrong, but it is important to be clear on the concentration of hydrogen peroxide – In your narration, you talk of “1%”.  Your chart, 8 minutes into your presentation, is labeled as "Proper Dosing for 1%".  However, in that chart you show the correct dilutions to arrive at “.1%” (i.e., one-tenth of 1%).  Aligned with the calculations in your chart, Dr. Mercola recommends .1% for use in a nebulizer.  Perhaps an even simpler way for you to arrive at .1% is to put 3 ml (equivalent to roughly 60 drops) of the saline solution into your nebulizer cup and then add just 2 drops of 3% hydrogen peroxide :)  [Dr. Brownstein, I should note, recommends an even more conservative .03%.  This would be one single drop of 3% hydrogen peroxide into 5 ml of saline solution. You can simply dispose of the approximate extra 2 ml in your cup when your 10-minute nebulizer treatment is up.]

title: Healing Powers of Essential Oils from the Bible Making a Comeback
text: No, the product today is NOT what was used in the Biblical days. Distilling came around 1200, and today's product is a more highly concentrated liquid. In fact the frankincense in the Christmas story was dried clumps of tree resin at 1X its value as found in nature. It was not some liquid concentrate at 100 X its value. When we concentrate anything we change its properties.  If the people of the Bible could see essential oils today, they would not recognize the product or how they are promoted for use.

title: Healing Powers of Essential Oils from the Bible Making a Comeback
text: For anyone who thinks our modern day distilling existed in ancient times, you probably also think Jesus drove a honda accord. The earliest record we have for distilling is around 900AD.  Back in the biblical days they were limited to clay pots. They didnt have stainless steel or 2019 tech. The product today is a highly concentrated hydrocarbon product with different properties. If you want essential oils, correction, OILS from biblical times, you will have to make them yourself by either cold pressing the oils with stones, or soaking herbs in a clay pot with olive oil. But stop kidding yourselves.

title: Use Oregano Essential Oil as a Natural Antibiotic
text:
The part of oil of oregano that you want to function as your antibiotic is called Carvacrol, it disrupts the membrane on the bacteria you mentioned in the video. The thymol is actually something you want very low value of in your oil. While carvacrol is non-toxic, thymol has a high degree of toxicity (to the heart and to the liver), so please be aware of that. Ideally, the levels should be less than 2%. In a good oil of oregano capsule (easiest way to take it, you get less than 2% thymol and more than 80% carvacrol. You want to purchase your oil from a company that tests each batch they make for the accurate percentages. Some oils can have more than 35% thymol and that is concentration is poisonous).
 Thymol is good against worms. Literally. It used to be the go to treatment for hookworm and today its used in preventing food spoilage because its great against mites and mold. Its also used as an antiseptic. Its also a good combo with antibiotics when fighting bacteria ingested with spoiled foodstuffs. However it is TOXIC to humans in high concentrations. Do NOT ingest oil of oregano high in percentage of Thymol. Zane Hellas is a good brand out of Greece. 
I Use a mouthwash for nipping things in the bud when my throat hurts. That also helps against gingivitis btw, but you cannot mix oil and water. Oil and water doesnt mix no matter how much you want it to. Use a prepared mouthwash so that no part of your gums are exposed to 100% strong oil of oregano, as it will give you a chemical burn. If you have Sibo, taking oil of oregano 7 days out of each month is a great addiction to IF and healthy Keto. It helped me rid mine. I do not recommend continuing on with the oil unless you have reason to believe you need to, its a strong natural antibiotic and it does interfere with your own biome. There is NO evidence, NONE whatsoever, to show that oil of oregano can distinguish between good bacteria and probiotic ones. It works by disolving the membranes of bacteria, and because of its INDISCRIMINATION, its actually a great oil for SIBO. But its not a smart nanite. It wont distinguish, and its a very alternative fact to say otherwise.


If you are looking for something more longterm and less disruptive to your own gut health, try taking curcumin with your meals. Its a wonderful antiinflammatory.

I had digestive problems since I was a child and as an adult I got candidiasis, a tapeworm, and then c. difficile. After all those were cleared I got ME/CFS. I had to do a lot of things to get better but the multi-strain probiotics were a game changer. Not just for my gut but overall I felt better, had more energy, less brain fog and less brain issues. It took one or two weeks of feeling crummy when I started the probiotics for that to go away and to feel good. But I know others report that probiotics messed them up. I dont know what the  difference is between those it helps and those it messes up other than histamine intolerance which I dont seem to have,  but maybe its because I didn't have SIBO or a bad bacteria overgrowth, being as how I cleared out those things, and probably had a lack of or a greatly reduced good bacteria population in my gut and so it took well to reseeding. Also they say that probiotics are only temporary and work only as long as you take them but I felt great for years after taking only one bottle of Equilibrium so I believe the good bacteria did take up residence unless there's another reason for it.

title: Probiotics Benefits + Myths | Improve Gut Health | Doctor Mike
text:
I started down the Probiotic/prebiotic journey recently along with a change in diet and have some recommendations for others thinking of doing the same.
Food that have probiotic - Kimchi, Sauerkraut (fermented, make sure it mentions that it has live cultures, making it is also pretty easy), kefir, apple cider vinegar, kombucha, fermented foods (such as fermented pickles, but be careful, most pickles are made with vinegar instead of via fermentation).
Prebiotic foods:
Asparagus, Onions, garlic, artichokes, leeks, kiwi, Root veggies such as sweet potatoes, squash wild yams, parsnips.

My nutritionist recommended having 2-3 servings a day of prebiotic and probiotic food and to try getting both (pre and probiotics) from foods and not supplements.

If you plan your day/diet out right, you can get a good prebiotic intake going with minimal effort. Try the foods listed above, keep the ones that you like on your diet. Also research! You can find more foods that are in pre/pro biotic food list.

At first you may get cramps, constipated, etc. But once your body gets used to probiotic intake, you will feel better than you did before (probably, unless there is something about you specifically which does not want the foods you are adding to your diet, see a doctor?).

One last thing, change your diet! Look up why you should be eating organic food vs non organic. Research the amount of pesticide used in non organic veggies and what you are putting into your body when you eat things sprayed with pesticides freely. Drink more water (16 cups a day!). and look into intermittent fasting. If you can add all of these things to your routine, I think you will see great improvement as did I. I lost 10 pounds in the last month and feel much better (not that I was feeling bad to begin with). Good luck! Comment if you have any questions.

I am not a doctor and this is not medical advice, just sharing my experience and advice I would give a friend trying to start a probiotic regiment.

title: TMG: The Only Legal Compound that Targets Fat Specifically
text:
The video discusses the strong human data on Trimethylglycine (TMG), a compound known for its potential to enhance fat loss without reducing lean body mass. TMG, also found in beets, acts as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and a methyl donor, which plays a role in DNA transcription and metabolic processes. Studies, including a systematic review and meta-analysis published in the journal *Nutrients*, show that TMG significantly reduces total fat mass and body fat percentage by about 2.44% without affecting muscle mass. In some cases, it even increases lean body mass.

TMG works by donating a methyl group to homocysteine, converting it into methionine, which is crucial for carnitine formation. Carnitine helps transport fat into mitochondria for oxidation, enhancing fat burning. Additionally, TMG improves insulin resistance by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, further supporting fat loss and muscle building. It also prevents the formation of white adipose tissue, potentially protecting against fat storage during caloric surpluses.

text:
I'm going to say this now, even though Chris is correct, as was Eby, there is something that people need to understand.

The rhinovirus cannot bind to cells properly, which is true, from the Zinc, but this accounts to Rhinovirus. Not all colds are Rhinovirus. This is why it doesn't work at all on some colds, even if you start right at the infection stage. You basically have a 50% chance of it working due to the nature of the common cold being around 50% Rhinovirus and 50% something else, but that 50% is like flipping a coin. I'd rather have a 50/50 chance of not being owned by a cold than 100% chance of that happening.

title: Can Vitamin C and Zinc Help Cure Colds?
text:
Speaking from some experiences as a singer - the zinc wasn't so much to "cure" the cold as to give my body some extra ammo for the fight, though obviously that's also not a scientific approach. Zicam doesn't claim it'll cure colds; they say it'll help shorten duration and make the symptoms less severe while you have a cold - and they stress taking their product within that first 24 hours as being important as well. (Not defending them: but this is why I used that product; no magic claims.)
At least some of the perceived effectiveness of these "cures" is due to the placebo effect, it seems. Of course I'm sure plenty of folks would challenge that too!

As for the herbal products falsifying information...I'm not sure how anyone can be surprised.

title: Common Cold/Flu Treatment that ACTUALLY work | 2025-2026 Recommendations
text:
Symptom & Medication Timestamps

03:27 - Prevention (Vitamin C):** Taking about 1,000 mg of Vitamin C daily may not prevent a cold, but it can shorten its duration if you do get sick.
03:59 - Prevention (Zinc):** Zinc may help reduce the likelihood of getting a cold. A daily dose of 10 mg is suggested for prevention. If you already have a cold, taking 10 mg every 3 hours within the first 48-72 hours of symptoms can help reduce the length of the cold.
05:54 - Aches, Pains & Fever (Acetaminophen):** A dose of 500-1,000 mg can help with muscle pain and fever, but you should not exceed 4,000 mg per day to avoid liver toxicity.
06:43 - Aches, Pains & Fever (Ibuprofen):** This also helps with fever and can reduce inflammation .
07:24 - Runny Nose (Antihistamines):** First-generation antihistamines like diphenhydramine (Benadryl) are recommended to stop nasal secretions  . The typical dose is 25-50 mg every 4 hours as needed.
08:29 - Stuffy Nose (Decongestants):** Pseudoephedrine (Sudafed) is considered the best option, though it requires an ID to purchase and can raise blood pressure. Oxymetazoline spray (Afrin) is also highly effective but should only be used for a maximum of three days.
09:35 - Cough (Suppressants):** Dextromethorphan can help suppress a cough but may interact with antidepressants. First-generation antihistamines like diphenhydramine can also sometimes help.
10:35 - Chest Congestion (Mucus):** A saline nebulizer is recommended over medications like Mucinex to help clear mucus from the lungs.
11:29 - Sore Throat (Numbing Sprays):** Phenol spray and Benzocaine spray are both effective for numbing a sore throat.

title: How to treat the Common Cold with things that ACTUALLY work!
text:
I'm a primary care doc. About the zinc:

- needs to be zinc lozenges (melts in mouth). The mechanism is presumed although not entirely confirmed to be from contact of zinc to your throat 

- must start it < 24 hours of symptom onset for it to work

- studies showed it reduced length of symptoms if taking > 75mg TOTAL / day. Most labels on the lozenges say to take 10-15mg every 2-3 hours, so you need to be consistent.
author: @Greatpics222

title: How to treat the Common Cold with things that ACTUALLY work!
text: I appreciate this channel sm! I work on a respiratory floor so here go my all time favorites: Tylenol for pain and fever. For a wet cough 3% saline through a nebulizer, and steam from a shower or humidifier. For a dry cough beekeepers naturals, benzonatate (prescribed). And for congestion: Neil med sinus rinse (use FILTERED water only), saline spray. “Big guns” would be Afrin nasal spray (use for only 3 days max or it will worsen congestion) and Sudafed from behind the counter (AVOID if you have heart issues or high blood pressure). Any concerns just run it with your local friendly pharmacist. My Mexican remedies would also call for Vicks vapor rub and tea with onion, honey and lemon 😊 Don’t forget your warm fluids! It helps preserve the cilia in your airways, thins mucus, and keeps you hydrated from water loss via fever & faster breathing during a cold/flu. Stay happy and healthy
